name: Monitoring Stack Deployment
description: Deploy and configure a full observability stack with metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and alerting rules.

inputs.name: target_infrastructure
inputs.description: Kubernetes cluster details or VM fleet specification for stack deployment.

inputs.name: observability_requirements
inputs.description: Metrics, logging, tracing, and alerting scope with retention policies.

inputs.name: dashboard_specs
inputs.description: Pre-defined dashboard layouts and key performance indicators to track.

inputs.name: alert_channels
inputs.description: Notification targets (Slack, PagerDuty, email) and escalation policies.

outputs.name: stack_manifests
outputs.description: Helm charts or Kubernetes manifests for the deployed monitoring components.

outputs.name: dashboard_configs
outputs.description: Provisioned dashboards with key metrics, SLO panels, and service maps.

outputs.name: alert_rules
outputs.description: Alerting rules with severity levels, thresholds, and notification routing.

outputs.name: validation_report
outputs.description: End-to-end verification that metrics flow, alerts fire, and dashboards render.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is monitoring stack deployment, not incident investigation or performance debugging.
Verify target cluster or infrastructure is accessible before deploying monitoring components.
Validate each deployed component emits metrics or logs before proceeding.
Return stack manifests, dashboards, alert rules, and validation report with access instructions.

steps.description: Analyze target infrastructure and define metrics, logs, and traces collection requirements.
Install Prometheus or equivalent metrics collector with service discovery and scrape configurations.
Deploy log aggregation (Loki, Fluentd, or equivalent) with structured log parsing and retention.
Install distributed tracing backend (Tempo, Jaeger) with OpenTelemetry collector configuration.
Provision Grafana or equivalent dashboards with SLO panels, service maps, and infrastructure overviews.
Define alerting rules, severity levels, notification channels, and escalation policies.
Verify metrics ingestion, log search, trace queries, dashboard rendering, and alert firing.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read Helm values, dashboard JSON templates, and alerting rule files.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write stack manifests, dashboard configs, and validation reports.

tools.name: shell.exec
tools.purpose: Execute helm, kubectl, and curl commands for deployment and verification.

verification.method: Prometheus targets are up and scraping within 2 minutes of deployment.
Log aggregation returns results for a test query within 5 minutes.
All provisioned dashboards load without errors and display live data.
A synthetic alert fires and routes to the configured notification channel.

success_criteria: Metrics, logs, and traces pipelines are operational end-to-end.
Dashboards render live data for all defined KPIs.
Alert rules are active and route to configured channels.
Validation report confirms all components pass health checks.

rollback.when: If end-to-end validation fails, uninstall monitoring components via Helm and restore prior state.
